_comment_phase1: Phase 1 uses rank-product method (Breitling et al. 2004) across 4 evidence layers: selection (MEME p-value), convergence (PhyloP), convergent AAs, expression (Bgee). Tiers assigned by FDR-corrected rank-product p-value.
_comment_phase1_tiers: Tier1: FDR < 0.05, Tier2: FDR < 0.20, Tier3: rest. Expression weight is small (0.10) to add tissue-specificity without overriding evolution signal.
_comment_phase2: Safety is NOT additive — it is a multiplicative floor. safety_floor=0.20 means any gene with safety_score < 0.20 is zeroed out regardless of evolutionary signal. The previous floor of 0.40 was too aggressive: a gene accumulating two independent moderate safety flags (e.g. hub_risk + modest GTEx breadth) would score ~0.35 and be zeroed, losing genes with strong evolutionary signal. 0.20 only eliminates genes with genuinely severe safety profiles (e.g. broadly essential + high PheWAS liability). structural (Step 9b) weight taken from druggability (0.18→0.13) — pocket proximity and AM score overlap with druggability evidence, so joint weight is still appropriate.

rank_product._comment: Phase 1 rank-product FDR thresholds for tier assignment
